input.background: Assume there are 10 identical units to place on test at the same time t = 0 where each unit has an exponential life distribution with a mean time to failure MTTF μ = 5 h and that the units operate independently.
input.question: Determine the expected and the variance of number of failures during the first hour, i.e. interval [0, 1).

output.answer: Let $N_1$ be the number of failures during the first hour, in the time interval $[0$, 1). The probability of failure of a unit in the time interval $[0,1)$ is $$ q_1=1-e^{- rac{1}{5}}=0.1813 $$ The distribution of $N_1$ that the probability of exactly $i$ failures in $[0,1)$ is $$ P\left(N_1=i\right)=\binom{10}{i}(0.1813)^i(0.8187)^{10-i} $$ The expected number of failures $\left(N_1\right)$ during the first hour is $$ E\left(N_1\right)=n q_1=10(0.1813)=1.813 $$ The variance of $N_1$ is $$ \begin{aligned} V\left(N_1\right) & =n q_1\left(1-q_1\right) \\ & =10(0.1813)(0.8187)=1.4843 \end{aligned} $$

meta_info.chapter: Chapter 2: Distribution Functions and Its Applications
